shortAnswer: Sodium (Na) is a vital electrolyte that regulates fluid balance and blood pressure. Low levels (hyponatremia) can cause confusion and seizures; high levels (hypernatremia) suggest dehydration. Discuss any deviations with your doctor.
metaDescription: Sodium elevated or low? Normal ranges, causes of electrolyte imbalance, and when to see a doctor: explained simply.
doctorQuestions: What is causing my sodium imbalance?
Should I adjust my fluid intake?
Could a medication be affecting my sodium level?
